title: Icicopsis caudata

The species is further categorized under the subclass Asteridae and the family Rubiaceae. At the level of the kingdom, it is classified as Plantae. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

intro: Icicopsis caudata, also known as the Himalayan Horse Chestnut, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Icicopsis caudata include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
